contact.linkedinLabel: Add my LinkedIn URL here later

web.title: I’m building my web portfolio around clean interfaces, responsive layouts, and steady full-stack growth.
web.intro: I’m still early in my web journey, but I take it seriously. I like turning ideas into usable pages, improving structure, and learning how frontend and backend work together in real products.
web.identity: I don’t want my web work to feel like random practice. I want it to show how I think, how I structure interfaces, and how I’m growing into full-stack development with intention.
web.about_title: I want my web page to feel focused, clear, and easy to scan.
web.about_body: When I build for the web, I care about spacing, readability, responsive behavior, and making the experience feel smooth instead of cluttered. I’m not trying to overstate my level. I’d rather show honest progress and solid improvement.
web.goal: My goal is to grow into a strong full-stack developer who can handle frontend quality, understand backend flows, and build products that feel polished for real users.
web.feature_title: This page itself is part of my web development proof.
web.feature_body: I rebuilt this portfolio so my web work has its own dedicated space, cleaner structure, and content that I can manage outside the page markup.

projects.title: Portfolio Rebuild
projects.summary: I redesigned my portfolio into separate web and game pages so each field has its own space and identity.

projects.title: Responsive UI Practice
projects.summary: I use responsive interface practice to improve layout control, spacing, readability, and cleaner visual flow.

projects.title: Full-Stack Learning Track
projects.summary: I’m actively learning backend structure and API-driven thinking so I can build stronger end-to-end projects over time.

game.title: I build game experiences around atmosphere, interaction, and playable ideas that people can actually try.
game.intro: Game development is where I feel strongest right now. I enjoy building prototypes, shaping mechanics, and creating atmosphere that supports the player experience from the first moment.
game.identity: I don’t want my game page mixed with my web page because the work, tools, and way I think about interaction are different. Here I want the focus to stay fully on games.
game.about_title: I want my game page to show playable proof, not just claims.
game.about_body: I like building projects where the player can feel movement, pacing, tension, and environment design working together. I’m most comfortable in Unity, and I also use Unreal for level design exploration.
game.goal: My goal is to keep improving as a game developer through stronger mechanics, better atmosphere, and more complete playable builds while expanding what I can ship publicly.
game.feature_title: This is the project that represents my game work best right now.
game.feature_name: The Happy Place: Smile of Deception
game.feature_body: I built this horror experience around tension, atmosphere, and escape. It reflects the side of my work that I currently feel most confident showing.

projects.title: The Happy Place: Smile of Deception
projects.summary: I built this psychological horror game around tension, dark atmosphere, and escape-driven storytelling.

projects.title: Knockout Party
projects.summary: I explored fast multiplayer obstacle-race gameplay with party-game energy and 3D movement.

projects.title: Save The Eggs
projects.summary: I built this 2D survival game around arcade-style timing, speed scaling, and score-based play.
